name: OZO Coffee

hours.note: Earliest open on Pearl Street. Verify current hours before visiting.

selloPersonality: Local roaster stamp, West Pearl anchor, specialty pour-overs

stamp.subLine: West Pearl's anchor roaster. The pour-over is the move.

story.headline: The roaster that anchors west Pearl Street.
story.body: OZO has been roasting in Boulder since 2007 and their West Pearl location is the natural start of the corridor. This is a serious coffee operation — single-origin pour-overs, ceremonial matcha, and a calm minimalist space that doesn't try too hard. The staff know their beans and will walk you through the options without making you feel tested.
story.insiderTip: Ask what single-origin pour-overs are on today. They'll let you smell the beans before you choose. The honey lavender latte has a following.
story.parkingNote: West Pearl Street parking. Metered weekdays, easier on weekends.

name: Amante Coffee

hours.note: Closes early on weekends. Morning stop only.

selloPersonality: Northern Italian cafe stamp, Walnut Street corner

stamp.subLine: A slice of Northern Italy on Walnut Street.

story.headline: Northern Italian coffee culture, one block off Pearl.
story.body: Amante brings an Italian sensibility to Boulder — the cortado is always on point, the outdoor seating is perfect for cyclists arriving from Canyon, and the vibe is calm without being precious. Just off Pearl on Walnut, it draws a mix of regulars and remote workers who know the tables are spaced right for actual work.
story.insiderTip: The outdoor seating faces the street — great for watching the Boulder cycling parade go by. The cortado is the order.
story.parkingNote: Walnut Street street parking. One block south of Pearl.

name: Trident Booksellers & Cafe

hours.note: Open every day. One of the longest hours on Pearl.

selloPersonality: OG Boulder stamp, bookstore cafe, no phones inside

stamp.subLine: The bookstore that made Pearl Street matter.

story.headline: The original Boulder coffee shop. Bookstore and cafe, inseparable.
story.body: Trident is OG Boulder. The bookstore came first — carefully curated, not algorithmic — and the cafe grew around it. Signs ask you not to use your phone inside. The covered patio works in the rain. The live music in back is a surprise the first time. This is the stop that tells you what Boulder actually values: books, coffee, conversation, and a little deliberate slowness.
story.insiderTip: Browse the used section before you order. The staff have opinions on the books and will share them. Happy hour on beer is real.
story.parkingNote: Pearl Street Mall — use the public parking structures on Walnut or Canyon.

name: The Laughing Goat
selloPersonality: Central Pearl stamp, open mic, Ethiopian coffee fable

stamp.subLine: Named after an Ethiopian coffee legend. The sidewalk is the point.

story.headline: The Pearl Street community hub. Named after a coffee fable.
story.body: The Laughing Goat takes its name from an Ethiopian legend about a goat herder who noticed his goats dancing after eating coffee berries — the origin story of coffee itself. The cafe earns that name: open mic sessions, glass doors that fold open onto Pearl Street in warm weather, a bulletin board that tells you more about Boulder than any guide. It's a living room for the corridor.
story.insiderTip: Check the bulletin board for events. The blueberry pastry is legitimately excellent. Dogs welcome on the patio.
story.parkingNote: Central Pearl Street. Use the public parking structures.

name: Süti & Co.

hours.note: Half block off Pearl on 16th. Closed Monday.

selloPersonality: Scandinavian cottage stamp, half block off Pearl, the detour worth making

stamp.subLine: The coziest half-block detour on the walk.

story.headline: A Scandinavian cottage, half a block off Pearl. The hygge stop.
story.body: Süti is in a converted brick cottage on 16th — you have to know to turn off Pearl to find it. Scandinavian decor, olive oil cakes baked in-house, Boxcar beans, acoustic piano some weekends. Female-owned and operated. It's the stop that rewards people who are actually walking rather than rushing. The vanilla, burnt honey, and cinnamon latte has a devoted following.
story.insiderTip: The orange cardamom latte is unlike anything else on the walk. The homewares inside are worth browsing — owner Andrea curates them. Go on a weekend morning for the full experience.
story.parkingNote: 16th Street, half block off Pearl. Street parking usually available.

name: Creature Comforts Cafe

hours.note: Closed Monday. Open until 6PM — the evening coffee stop.

selloPersonality: No-tip cafe stamp, coffee and wine, the evening pivot

stamp.subLine: Coffee until 6. Then wine. No tips — they pay their people right.

story.headline: The cafe that transitions into evening. No tipping, fair wages.
story.body: Creature Comforts was one of the first Boulder cafes to go gratuity-included — they pay a fair wage and price accordingly. The miso cardamom latte is genuinely inventive. Open until 6PM most days, it's the natural pivot point between the afternoon coffee walk and an evening on Pearl. Beer and wine after 4PM make it the only stop on the passport that earns two visits in one day.
story.insiderTip: The no-tip policy is real and the staff appreciate you knowing it. The miso cardamom latte is the order. Come back after 4PM for wine.
story.parkingNote: Central Pearl Street. Public parking structures nearby.

name: Rosetta Hall

hours.note: Food hall — coffee in morning, full food and bar all day. Rooftop by reservation.

selloPersonality: Food hall stamp, rooftop Pearl Street views, international stalls

stamp.subLine: The rooftop is the move. International food stalls, one building.

story.headline: Boulder's food hall. Rooftop views of Pearl Street, international stalls inside.
story.body: Rosetta Hall is the social hub of the Pearl Street corridor — a food hall with international stalls, a serious whiskey bar, and a rooftop with lounge beds and fire pits. It gets clubby late on weekends but daytime is relaxed and excellent for a proper lunch between coffee stops. The cucumber salad and the cocktails are the consistent highlights.
story.insiderTip: The rooftop requires a reservation on weekends but is often walk-in on weekdays. The whiskey selection is exceptional — ask what's open. Public parking across the street is $3 and free on weekends.
story.parkingNote: Public parking structure across Walnut St. $3 weekdays, free weekends.

name: Boxcar Coffee

hours.note: Closes early — morning and midday only. Short Monday hours.

selloPersonality: Precision roaster stamp, East Pearl, shared space with Cured

stamp.subLine: Precision coffee. The cinnamon roll is croissant dough. Order it.

story.headline: Boulder's most serious coffee shop. Shared with a gourmet sandwich counter.
story.body: Boxcar is where you go when you actually care about coffee. Own roasts, precision extraction, ethically sourced beans, and a baking program that produces one of the best cinnamon rolls in Colorado — croissant dough, not brioche, with cream cheese frosting that earns its place. Shares the space with Cured, a gourmet meat and cheese counter. The seasonal Black Velvet drink has a following.
story.insiderTip: Get the cinnamon roll. Get the seasonal special. The Tuesday morning run group is a Boulder institution — don't be surprised by a crowd of cyclists.
story.parkingNote: East Pearl Street. Street parking or the public structures back west.

name: Rapha Boulder

hours.note: Open daily. Group rides several times per week — check schedule.

selloPersonality: Cycling club cafe stamp, East Pearl, espresso and kit

stamp.subLine: Cycling club meets cafe. The espresso is serious. The kit is expensive.

story.headline: Part cycling club, part cafe, part clothing store. Very Boulder.
story.body: Rapha Clubhouse is where Boulder's cycling culture and coffee culture become the same thing. The espresso is genuine, the space is warm and well-lit, the staff know the roads, and the weekly group rides leave from here. You can work here, watch race footage on the screen, or just sit with a cortado and appreciate that only in Boulder does this combination make complete sense.
story.insiderTip: Check the ride calendar online — even if you're not cycling, the club energy is worth experiencing. The wifi is reliable. The staff will tell you which roads are worth riding.
story.parkingNote: East Pearl Street, next door to Boxcar. Share the same stretch.

name: Fresh Baked

hours.note: Open daily. Dual stamp — coffee passport and dispensary layer.

selloPersonality: East Pearl terminus stamp, OG 2010 dispensary, dual layer anchor

stamp.subLine: East Pearl end of the line. OG Boulder since 2010. Dual stamp.

story.headline: The eastern anchor of Pearl Street. Boulder's original dispensary since 2010.
story.body: Fresh Baked has held the east end of Pearl Street since 2010 — founded in the medical era, converted to recreational in 2014 when Colorado legalized. The converted house it occupies feels like it was always meant for this. It's the natural end of the Pearl Street walk and earns two stamps: coffee passport stop 10 and the dispensary layer anchor. Tax-included pricing, knowledgeable staff, consistent quality.
story.insiderTip: All prices are tax-included — what you see is what you pay. The loyalty program is worth signing up for if you're in Boulder regularly. The chandelier at the entrance is worth noticing.
story.parkingNote: East end of Pearl — street parking more available out here than central Pearl.

name: The Trust Cannabis Company

hours.note: Open daily. Most central Pearl Street dispensary.

selloPersonality: Central Pearl dispensary stamp, daily deals, heady brands

stamp.subLine: Corner of Pearl and 16th. Quality without overcharging.

story.headline: The most convenient dispensary on Pearl Street. Quality without the markup.
story.body: Tucked on Pearl and 16th, The Trust is the dispensary you find mid-walk — right between the coffee corridor and the east end. Daily deals, heady brands like Allgreens and Green Dot Labs, and budtenders who know the difference between what you say you want and what you actually want. Medical side available in addition to recreational.
story.insiderTip: Check the daily deals online before you walk in — they run some of the best in Boulder. The Silver shelf is notably strong for the price.
story.parkingNote: Central Pearl Street. Public parking structures a short walk.

name: The Health Center

hours.note: Verify current hours. Canyon Blvd, just off Pearl.

selloPersonality: Canyon Blvd anchor stamp, longtime independent, loyal regulars

stamp.subLine: Canyon Blvd's longtime independent. Regulars drive past 15 others to get here.

story.headline: The dispensary regulars drive past 15 others to reach.
story.body: The Health Center on Canyon Blvd has the kind of loyal customer base that tells you everything — people pass multiple dispensaries to get here. The staff are genuinely friendly, the product is consistently strong, and the value is real. It's the west-end anchor of the dispensary layer, close to Eben Fine Park and the Boulder Creek trailhead.
story.insiderTip: Ask staff what's fresh this week — they're honest about it. The holiday specials are legitimately good deals.
story.parkingNote: Canyon Blvd. More parking available than central Pearl Street.

name: Maikoh Holistics

hours.note: Closes earlier than others — plan accordingly. OG Boulder location.

selloPersonality: Connoisseur stamp, pesticide-free, the destination dispensary

stamp.subLine: Pesticide-free, connoisseur flower. People drive an hour for this.

story.headline: The connoisseur's dispensary. People drive an hour past other shops to get here.
story.body: Maikoh is a destination — the kind of place where the flower selection is updated constantly and smelling each jar is encouraged. Pesticide-free concentrates, thoughtfully sourced, and a quiet vibe that doesn't feel like a retail operation. The OG Boulder location. Reviews consistently say regulars drive from Denver to shop here rather than going to the dispensary around the corner.
story.insiderTip: Go early — it closes earlier than most Boulder dispensaries. The rosin selection is the draw for concentrate enthusiasts. Browse the menu online before visiting.
story.parkingNote: North Boulder off Glenwood Dr. Easy parking — not downtown.

name: Eben Fine Park

hours.note: Western trailhead of Boulder Creek Path. Gateway to Boulder Canyon.

selloPersonality: Western trailhead stamp, creek access, canyon gateway

stamp.subLine: Western trailhead. The creek starts here. Boulder Canyon begins.

story.headline: Where the Boulder Creek Path begins and the canyon opens up.
story.body: Eben Fine Park is the western anchor of the Boulder Creek Path — 5.5 miles of paved multi-use trail that runs east through downtown and out the other side. From here you can wade in the creek, watch tubers in summer, or walk up into Boulder Canyon. Cottonwoods, picnic tables, creek access, and a path that delivers you to Pearl Street with a proper sense of arrival.
story.insiderTip: Come early on summer weekends before the tubers and parking crowd arrive. The path east to Pearl Street takes about 20 minutes on foot — arrive via trail, stamp Pearl Street stops, walk back. That's the Boulder day.
story.parkingNote: Small lot at Arapahoe Ave. Fills fast on summer weekends — arrive by 9AM or use transit.

name: Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse

hours.note: Weekend brunch from 8AM. On the Boulder Creek Path corridor.

selloPersonality: Creek path midpoint stamp, Tajik gift, handcrafted in Dushanbe

stamp.subLine: A gift from Dushanbe, Tajikistan. The most beautiful building on the path.

story.headline: A gift from Boulder's sister city in Tajikistan. The jewel of the Creek Path.
story.body: The Dushanbe Teahouse was handcrafted by artisans in Dushanbe, Tajikistan — Boulder's sister city — and reassembled here piece by piece. The interior is extraordinary: hand-painted ceilings, carved columns, ceramic panels. It sits right on the Boulder Creek Path corridor and earns a stop whether you're walking the creek or just on Pearl. The tea selection is serious and the food is genuinely excellent.
story.insiderTip: Sit at the bar if you want to hear the story of the building from the staff — they know it well. Weekend brunch from 8AM is worth the trip. The picked salad and the mousse are the consistent highlights.
story.parkingNote: 13th St near Canyon. Easier parking than central Pearl. Short walk to the creek path.

name: Chautauqua Park

hours.note: Shuttle available summer weekends. Parking fills by 9AM on busy days.

selloPersonality: Flatirons trailhead stamp, the iconic Boulder hike, meadow and views

stamp.subLine: The Flatirons. The meadow. The hike that defines Boulder.

story.headline: The trail that defines Boulder. The Flatirons front and center.
story.body: Chautauqua is where every visit to Boulder eventually arrives. The Chautauqua Trail cuts straight through the historic meadow toward the Flatirons — 1.2 miles out and back, or a gateway to the full trail network beyond. The Dining Hall has been here since 1898. The cabins still rent. The summer concert series at the auditorium is one of the best outdoor music venues in Colorado.
story.insiderTip: Go on a weekday to avoid parking chaos. The shuttle runs summer weekends from downtown — take it. Gregory Canyon Trail is quieter than the main Chautauqua Trail if you want fewer people. Check the concert calendar.
story.parkingNote: Main lot by the auditorium fills fast summer weekends. Street parking on Baseline. Shuttle recommended on busy days.

name: Chautauqua Dining Hall

hours.note: Open daily from 8AM. Pre- and post-hike dining. Historic 1898 building.

selloPersonality: Historic dining hall stamp, 1898, bison burger, post-hike anchor

story.headline: The 1898 dining hall at the base of the Flatirons. The post-hike anchor.
story.body: The Chautauqua Dining Hall has been feeding hikers since 1898 — the building is original, the vibe is classic Colorado, and the bison burger is the reason people skip breakfast before the trail. The butternut squash shrimp pasta is the consistent dinner order. Open from 8AM for pre-hike coffee and breakfast, and stays open until 9PM for a proper meal after you come back down.
story.insiderTip: The bison burger is the order. First time eating bison — this is the place to do it. Ask the bartender for the building's history if you get Aspen. Walk-ins usually accommodated for lunch.
story.parkingNote: Same lot as Chautauqua Park trailhead. Arrive early or take the shuttle.
